{{Quote|For an unintelligent machine, the I.D.A. seems to have a mind of its own. Poor R2-D7 has a hard time getting it to work.|Complete Guide to Star Tours|<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours">''[[Complete Guide to Star Tours]]''</ref>}}
'''R2-D7''' was a conical-headed [[R4 astromech droid/Legends|R4 astromech droid]] who worked at the [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] travel agency's [[Tomorrowland Starport]], tending to [[StarSpeeder 3000]]s and operating the [[Inventory Droid Assistant]].<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" />

==Biography==
By [[4 ABY/Legends|4.3 ABY]],<ref name="KOTHB">According to {{Blog|holocron|7|Death Star timeline}}, the events of the [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] can be dated to [[4 ABY/Legends|4.3 ABY]].</ref> R2-D7 tended to [[StarSpeeder 3000]]s<ref name="CSWE" /> at the [[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]] travel agency's [[Tomorrowland Starport]]. Using its [[Scomp link/Legends|scomp link]], the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] also operated the track control<ref name="Star Tours" /> for the [[Inventory Droid Assistant]] to bring replacement parts from inventory to the [[Spaceport/Legends|starport's]] [[Droidnostics Center]]<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" /> under a [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] [[Unidentified Star Tours droid supervisor|supervisor]].<ref name="Star Tours" /> However, the I.D.A. seemed to have a mind of its own and R2-D7 had a hard time getting it to work.<ref name="Complete Guide to Star Tours" />

==Characteristics==
R2-D7 was a conical-headed,<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Class two droid/Legends|second-degree]]<ref name="TNEGTD">According to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] are classified as [[Class two droid/Legends|class two droid]]s.</ref> [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]]<ref name="CSWE" /> with a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptor]] and white plating, featuring a silver and teal trim. It was also equipped with a scomp link.<ref name="Star Tours" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:R2 D7.jpg|250px|thumb|R2-D7 at work]]
R2-D7 was originally created for the<ref name="Star Tours" /> [[1987]]<ref name="Imagineering Star Tours">{{SW|url=news/imagineering-star-tours-part-3-visual-effects-and-disney-magic|text=Imagineering Star Tours, Part 3: Visual Effects (and Disney) Magic|nolive=1}}</ref> [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] [[Wikipedia:Simulator ride|simulator ride]] [[Wikipedia:Amusement park|theme park]] attraction [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']]. It was featured in the ride's queue at each of its locations, which included each of Disney's resorts excluding [[Hong Kong Disneyland]] and [[Shanghai Disneyland]].<ref name="Star Tours" /> The droid was first identified by a "Spot the Droid" poster displayed at [[Tokyo Disneyland]]'s ''Star Tours'' attraction.<ref name="Retro-Tokyo!">{{EndorExpress|url=2013/05/retro-tokyo-a-look-back-at-tdls-star-tours-before-it-was-updated|text=Retro-Tokyo! A look back at TDL's Star Tours before it was updated…}}</ref>

An action figure of R2-D7 was released in [[2002]] as part of the [[Wikipedia:Walt Disney Parks and Resorts|Disney Parks]] exclusive [[Star Wars: Star Tours (toy line)|''Star Wars: Star Tours'']] toy line. However, it was misidentified as [[R4-M9/Legends|R4-M9]],<ref name="R4-M9 Star Tours">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Star Tours (toy line)|pack=R4-M9|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/potjstr4m9.asp}}</ref> an astromech droid whose 2002 ''[[Star Wars: Power of the Jedi]]'' action figure the former was recolored from.<ref name="R4-M9 Power of the Force">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: Power of the Jedi|pack=R4-M9|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/potjr4m9.asp}}</ref>
